rulings.Case Title: JOHN A KITHAS VS. LARRY WONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 8, 2023 line 1. Any appearance by the parties requesting an interpreter must be in-person. An interpreter has been reserved for a simultaneous translation for this hearing. If the party does not intend to appear at the hearing and the services of an interpreter are not needed, the party MUST notify the Court (via phone) no later than 4:00 p.m. on February 7, 2023. RESPONDENT ANNA WONG Homestead Exemption Declaration is DENIED. Respondent provides no relevant evidence to support any claim of surprise, mistake or excusable neglect for the judgment debtors not to appear at the September 14, 2022 hearing re: sale of dwelling individually, Larry Wong and Anna Wong, or via their counsel, Ester Adut. Judgement debtor had notice of the continued hearing re: sale of dwelling and could appear in-person (previously appeared in-person), via Zoom (previously appeared via Zoom) or properly via CourtCall. No explanation is provided for failure to appear via Zoom (video or phone). Additionally, the Court's order after September 14, 2022 hearing re: sale of dwelling is based on multiple briefs filed over a period of 4 months. The written submissions discussed the homestead exemption and the ruling on September 14, 2022 necessarily included a ruling on the homestead claim as is evidenced by the final order. The ruling at September 14, 2022 hearing was based on multiple written submissions of the parties for which the Court granted several continuances to make sure that both sides had ample opportunity to present their evidence and arguments in writing. There is no evidence provided to support a possibly different outcome (given extensive briefing and several continued hearings) had defendant appeared in-person, over Zoom (video or phone) or properly over the CourtCall instead of a CourtCall line designated for non-participating observers (The Court's web-site clearly states: "San Francisco Superior Court has established listen-only telephone lines to allow remote access to public court proceedings."), which is apparently the number respondent's counsel used to appear at the hearing. As stated above, the Court continued prior hearings re: sale of dwelling, at which the judgment debtor was present, specifically for the purpose of giving everyone every opportunity to present their evidence and arguments in writing and to give the parties more time to collect such evidence. The Court further notes that it appears that no notice that the tentative ruling for September 14, 2022 was contested. The Court's September 14, 2022 tentative ruling, based on voluminous written submissions, was uncontested. Therefore, on the current request for relief via Homestead Exemption Declaration, the Court rules that the determination of the Court based on evidence, briefs and oral arguments submitted to the Court before September 14, 2022 should not be modified. Furthermore, contrary to respondents' argument in the reply, the hearing on the judgement Debtor's Homestead Exemption Declaration was set within 20 days of Sheriff's delivery of the Declaration to the Court on January 23, 2023. Respondent provides no evidence that the Declaration was delivered to the Court by the Sheriff at anytime prior to January 23, 2023. The Court further notes that the issue of the homestead exemption, its applicability and its effect on the sale of dwelling requests has been addressed by this Court on multiple occasions before. Therefore, any future challenges in this Court to the Court's determination that the sale of dwelling shall proceed, may be considered frivolous per CCP 128.5/128.7. =(501/CFH) END OF 1 OF 2

rulings.Case Title: JOHN A KITHAS VS. LARRY WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: RESPONDENT ANNA WONG HOMESTEAD EXEMPTION DECLARATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 8, 2023 line 1. RESPONDENT ANNA WONG HOMESTEAD EXEMPTION DECLARATION (for tentative ruling purposes only) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear. END 2 OF 2

rulings.Case Title: CHINA LIVE VENTURES LIMITED, L.P. VS. 644 BROADWAY LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 8, 2023 line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT CHINA LIVE VENTURES LIMITED, L.P., CHINA LIVE, LLC DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Cross-Complaint to allege facts in support of each element of the causes of action for breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing, so that these causes of action are not duplicative of the causes of action for breach of contract. Demurrer is also sustained with leave to amend as to declaratory relief causes of action to allege an actual controversy regarding future rights and obligations of the parties that are not addressing the past wrongs (breach of contract) and will not be necessarily addressed in the causes of action for breach of contract (e.g. in order to establish that the cross-complaintant breached a contractual provision to allow access to the books, the trier of fact will have to determine that such contractual provision exists and cross-complainant has a right to access the books). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
